field.description: Dot-notation path into the webhook payload (e.g., 'check_run.conclusion').
field.title: Field

operator.description: Comparison operator. Unary operators (not_empty, is_empty) ignore value.
operator.title: Operator

value.description: Expected value. Required for binary operators (eq, neq, in, not_in, contains). Ignored for unary operators (not_empty, is_empty).
value.title: Value

TriggerConditionSchema.title: TriggerConditionSchema

TriggerDefinitionSchema.description:
A single trigger definition within a triggers.json file.

Defines when a workflow should fire and how webhook payload fields
map to workflow input variables.

description.description: What this trigger does and when it fires.
description.title: Description

conditions.description: Conditions that must ALL match for the trigger to fire. Empty list means the trigger fires on every event of this type.
conditions.title: Conditions

input_mapping.description: Maps workflow input names to dot-notation paths into the webhook payload. Example: {'pr_number': 'pull_request.number', 'branch': 'pull_request.head.ref'}
input_mapping.title: Input Mapping

TriggerDefinitionSchema.title: TriggerDefinitionSchema

description:
Root schema for triggers.json — the distributable trigger definition format.

Each workflow in a plugin package can include a triggers.json file that
defines when the workflow should automatically fire in response to GitHub events.
Triggers are attached to repos at install time via `syn triggers create --from-package`.

Source of truth for schemas/plugin/triggers.schema.json.
See ADR-052 for the schema generation strategy.

triggers.description: One or more trigger definitions for this workflow.
triggers.title: Triggers

title: TriggerFileSchema
